message:

@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "atlantis-686-allow-supplying-wildcard-for-dir",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "atlantis: Allow supplying wildcard for dir",
+    "description": "Allow supplying wildcard for dir. Requested by 93+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current atlantis deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your atlantis version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis\nhelm list -n atlantis 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working atlantis installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review atlantis configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant atlantis configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis\nkubectl get configmap -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=atlantis\n```\nWe define our terraform code in what we call stacks. Each stack defines multiple environments where each directory off root is the environment.\n\nCurrently we have to add a project for each directory."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Allow supplying wildcard for dir",
+        "description": "Can you describe how it would solve the issue?\n\nThe use-cases here are (and please correct me if I'm wrong)\n\n1. Using `atlantis plan -d 'prod/*'` to run all projects in `prod/*` space\n2. Using `atlantis.yaml` to define wildcards for specific projects\n    ```yaml\n    version: 3\n    projects:\n    -\n```yaml\nlocals {\nworkflow = \"someworkflow\"\n}\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade atlantis to include the fix",
+        "description": "If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade atlantis:\n```bash\nhelm repo update\nhelm upgrade atlantis atlantis/atlantis --namespace atlantis\n```\nVerify the upgrade:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n atlantis\nhelm list -n atlantis\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis\nkubectl get events -n atlantis --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Allow supplying wildcard for dir\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Can you describe how it would solve the issue?\n\nThe use-cases here are (and please correct me if I'm wrong)\n\n1. Using `atlantis plan -d 'prod/*'` to run all projects in `prod/*` space\n2. Using `atlantis.yaml` to define wildcards for specific projects\n    ```yaml\n    version: 3\n    projects:\n    - dir: dir1/**\n      workflow: default\n    - dir: dir2/**\n      workflow: terragrunt\n    ```",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "locals {\nworkflow = \"someworkflow\"\n}",
+        "locals {\n  atlantis_workflow = \"someworkflow\"\n}",
+        "workflows:\n  someworkflow:\n    apply:\n      steps:\n      ......."
+      ]
+    }
+  },
